$comment: Machine-checkable security claims for CardToken. Every trust claim made in TOKEN_LAUNCH_STRATEGY.md and the contract natspec must appear here, mapped to executable evidence. Run `npm run verify` — it fails if any claim's evidence is missing, stale, or failing. Do not edit a claim without editing the code/tests that back it, and vice versa.

claims.statement: Total supply is exactly 250,000,000 CARD, minted once at deployment to the deployer.

evidence.title: mints the full fixed supply to the deployer

claims.statement: No code path can ever increase or decrease the supply: there is no mint, burn, or rebase function.
claims.source: CardToken.sol natspec; TOKEN_LAUNCH_STRATEGY.md § Launch Sequence step 1

claims.statement: Transfers can never be paused or frozen.
No address can be blacklisted or blocked from transferring.
claims.source: CardToken.sol natspec; TOKEN_LAUNCH_STRATEGY.md § Launch Sequence step 1

claims.statement: Transfers move exactly the requested amount; there is no tax, fee, or skim.
claims.source: CardToken.sol natspec; TOKEN_LAUNCH_STRATEGY.md § Launch Sequence step 1
claims.notes: The CardTokenHandler additionally asserts exact-amount deltas inside every fuzzed transfer/transferFrom sequence.

claims.statement: Ownership grants no power over the token: the only state-changing functions are the standard ERC-20 surface plus the two Ownable handover functions, none of which touch balances or supply.
renounceOwnership() sets the owner to the zero address, permanently and verifiably on-chain.

evidence.title: renounces ownership to the zero address

claims.statement: A transfer exceeding the sender's balance always reverts.

evidence.title: reverts when transferring more than the sender's balance
